name: Cloudflare User-Agent Anomaly Blocklist Starter Pack
description: Detect and mitigate clearly malicious or scanner-like User-Agent patterns (including empty UA and common exploit tooling identifiers). Start in log/challenge mode to tune before any block enforcement.
objective: Reduce automated reconnaissance and exploit probing traffic that advertises high-risk User-Agent fingerprints.
risk_mitigated: Scanner-led vulnerability discovery, opportunistic exploit attempts, and noisy abusive automation.

rules.name: Empty or missing User-Agent
rules.description: Flags requests without a User-Agent header. Common in low-effort scanners and scripted abuse.

rules.name: Known scanner or exploit-tool User-Agent signatures
rules.expression: (lower(http.user_agent) contains "sqlmap" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "nikto" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "nmap" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "masscan" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "acunetix" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "nessus" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "openvas" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "wpscan" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "gobuster" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "dirbuster" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "ffuf" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "zgrab" or lower(http.user_agent) contains "whatweb")
rules.description: Challenges traffic that self-identifies as widely used scanning or exploit tooling.

deployment_notes: Phase 1 (recommended): keep empty-UA rule in log mode and scanner-signature rule in managed_challenge mode.
Phase 2: review 7-14 days of security events; only move specific high-confidence signatures to block after validating no business automation impact.
Prefer scoped exceptions (source IP/service account/path) over broad User-Agent allowlists.

Minimal Terraform example (Cloudflare custom rule expression): resource "cloudflare_ruleset" "ua_anomaly" { kind = "zone" phase = "http_request_firewall_custom" name = "ua-anomaly" zone_id = var.zone_id rules { action = "managed_challenge" expression = "(lower(http.user_agent) contains \"sqlmap\" or lower(http.user_agent) contains \"nikto\")" description = "Challenge known scanner UAs" enabled = true } }
tuning_notes: Do not immediately block all empty User-Agent traffic; some uptime monitors, legacy integrations, or internal health checks may omit UA.
Start with managed_challenge for scanner signatures to reduce false positives from authorized security testing.
If your org performs sanctioned scanning, exempt known scanner source ranges or authenticated test paths instead of disabling the rule globally.
Correlate hits with ASN, request rate, URI entropy, and threat score before escalating to block.
Periodically review signature list and remove stale terms that generate benign traffic in your environment.

dashboards: Cloudflare Security Events: filter by ruleset name and rule ID
Top User-Agent strings triggering cf-ua-anomaly-* rules
Challenge solve rate and repeat offender IPs
alerts: Spike in empty User-Agent matches over baseline
Sustained scanner-signature events from single ASN or /24
Drop in challenge solve rate indicating non-human automated probing
